mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and eases financial conditions, which lifts S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when the policy rate is already near the lower bound and continues to decline.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above the prior 3-month pace while FEDFUNDS is still low, real rates fall and policy is perceived as behind the curve, supporting equities over the next 5 trading days through a liquidity/risk-on reaction.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above 3.0% while FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce positive 5-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the average return after triggers is not above the unconditional 5-day mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates deteriorating household income and weaker future demand, which depresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as analysts revise earnings lower.
failure_condition: Reject if a 3-month rise in UNRATE does not coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if the sign of the rolling 3-year correlation turns persistently positive.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and fall in UNRATE signals broad payroll expansion and tighter labor conditions, which supports consumption and corporate revenue, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises for 3 consecutive months and UNRATE falls by at least 0.2 pp over the same period
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes fail to deliver positive 60-day SPX returns with frequency above 55%, or if PAYEMS gains without UNRATE improvement do not lose predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in RSAFS captures stronger retail spending and near-term household demand, which should improve sales expectations and lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect weakens if sales growth is entirely inflation-driven rather than volume-driven.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth exceeds CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation by at least 1.0 pp
failure_condition: Reject if high-RSAFS-growth episodes do not outperform low-growth episodes on 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal disappears after deflating sales by CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO reflects improving industrial capacity utilization and cyclical production momentum, which feeds into stronger earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if monthly increases in INDPRO do not correspond to positive 5-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if the estimated effect is unstable across rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST after a prior elevated level signals housing demand deterioration and tighter private credit transmission, which should pressure cyclical equities and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 8% over 3 months after being above its 12-month median
failure_condition: Reject if post-trigger 60-day SPX returns are not negative on average, or if the trigger frequency does not produce a materially lower return distribution than non-trigger periods.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated UNRATE and weak INDPRO identifies a policy-sensitive slowdown in which bad growth news dominates easier-rate expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails when inflation is also falling quickly and the market treats weakness as growth-supportive for policy easing.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is above 9.0% and INDPRO is below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show negative 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power beyond UNRATE alone.
